name: ws17: Democritus — verify idle-graph gate keeps net_change suppression on real daemon (post-merge)
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T1, D12)
- `crates/clawft-kernel/src/cognitive_tick.rs::run_democritus_loop`
- `.weftos/runtime/kernel.log` (lines 10538-11220, 2026-04-28 10:47 → 15:36 UTC)

## Problem / gap

In this audit session, the Democritus stuck-loop bug — where `kernel.log` showed continuous `Stuck { net_change: 0.0 }` warnings on geometric backoff — was fixed by an idle-graph gate added to `cognitive_tick.rs` (uncommitted at audit time, but tested 23/23 locally). The fix needs post-merge verification on a real daemon to confirm the idle-graph gate actually suppresses the `net_change == 0.0` warning chain in production conditions, and that the loop still emits non-trivial signals when the causal graph becomes active.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Idle-graph gate change merged to `development-0.7.0` (or release branch) and built via `scripts/build.sh native`
- [ ] Run a real daemon for at least 30 minutes; verify `kernel.log` shows no `DEMOCRITUS: still stuck after N checks: Stuck { net_change: 0.0 }` chain when graph is idle
- [ ] Drive activity into the causal graph (e.g. via a brief agent loop session) and confirm Democritus emits non-`Stuck` states
- [ ] Add or extend an integration test in `crates/clawft-kernel/tests/` that exercises the idle-graph gate explicitly
- [ ] Update `.planning/development_notes/sprint-16/democritus-loop.md` with verification notes

## Dependencies
- Idle-graph gate fix in `cognitive_tick.rs` (this session, uncommitted)
- ECC + EML coherence shipped in K3c / sprint-16

## Notes
Do NOT re-file the original stuck-loop bug — it was fixed in this session. This task only verifies the fix on a real (non-test-harness) daemon. P1 carry-over from D12 is now closed by code; this is its post-merge gate.

name:
ws17: ECC — wire boot_ecc() runtime function into Kernel

boot sequence
description_md:
## Source - `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T2, D1) - `.planning/development_notes/k3c-ecc-integration.md` - `crates/clawft-kernel/src/{causal,crossref,impulse,hnsw_service,calibration,cognitive_tick}.rs` ## Problem / gap `boot_ecc()` was scoped in K3c but never wired into `Kernel

` boot. Resource-tree namespaces under `/kernel/services/ecc/` and the tool catalog are populated, but service instances (HnswService, CausalGraph, CognitiveTick) and calibration are not actually executed at boot. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] Decide fold-vs-fork (see Q1 / T41) and document in an ADR or note - [ ] Implement `boot_ecc()` that constructs HnswService, CausalGraph, CognitiveTick and wires them into `Kernel

` - [ ] Calibration runs at boot and stays under the 100ms budget - [ ] All 562 ECC tests still pass; new boot path covered by at least one integration test - [ ] Update `weftos/ecc.mdx` if user-visible behavior shifts ## Dependencies - ECC feature flag, BootPhase::Ecc already landed ## Notes Fold-vs-fork decision (T41 / Q1) is a prerequisite. Out of scope for 0.7.0 release gate.

name: ws17: ECC — verify ecc feature exclusion on wasm32-unknown-unknown
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T3, D2)

## Problem / gap

The `ecc` feature pulls native deps (blake3, vector-memory) that won't link on `wasm32-unknown-unknown`. Exclusion is asserted in cfg gates but not verified by CI or local build.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh browser` succeeds with default features
- [ ] Add an explicit assertion (CI step or doc) that `--features ecc` is rejected for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`
- [ ] Update `crates/clawft-wasm/Cargo.toml` if any dep coupling needs to be broken

## Dependencies
- WASM build infrastructure (Stream 13)

## Notes
Small but real risk for browser builds; stream-13 cross-cut.

name: ws17: ECC — add governance gates to auth_service rotate_credential, request_token, revoke_token
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T4, D14, D15, D16)
- `.planning/development_notes/governance-certification.md`

## Problem / gap

`auth_service:rotate_credential` and `auth_service:request_token` are chain-logged but **not** governance-gated. Any agent with IPC access could rotate any credential without a policy check; token issuance is gated only by the credential's `allowed_agents` list. `auth_service:revoke_token` is partial (logged, not gated).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add `GovernanceGate::evaluate()` calls in front of all three auth_service methods
- [ ] Wire `EffectVector` with explicit risk dimensions (not context-only)
- [ ] Add policy expressions covering rotate/request/revoke and document in `governance-certification.md`
- [ ] Add tests covering both allowed and denied paths
- [ ] Re-run governance certification audit and update the 19-gate count

## Dependencies
- Existing `GovernanceEngine` and `GateBackend` infrastructure
- Cross-cuts with Stream 8 (security)

## Notes
Closely tied to T43 (rotate-but-not-revoke policy expression).

name: ws17: governance — make EffectVector explicit on auth/config/a2a/cron gates
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T5, D17)

## Problem / gap

`EffectVector` on the auth, config, a2a, and cron gates is currently 'context-only' / heuristic — risk weighting is implicit. Decisions are still being made by the engine, but the inputs aren't auditable.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Define an explicit `EffectVector` schema for each of the four gates (dimensions + weights)
- [ ] Refactor gate construction sites to populate the vector explicitly
- [ ] Add tests pinning the dimensions per gate type
- [ ] Update `governance-certification.md`

## Dependencies
- T4 (governance gates on auth_service) overlaps

## Notes
Carry-over to 0.8.x hardening pass. Cross-cuts Stream 8.

name: ws17: weave — implement weaver ecc CLI subcommands
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T6, D4)

## Problem / gap

No CLI surface yet for ECC operations. Operators have to talk to the resource tree directly. `weaver ecc {status, calibrate, predict, tick, ...}` is sketched in K3c notes but not implemented.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add `weaver ecc` subcommand group to `clawft-weave`
- [ ] Cover at least: status, calibrate, predict, coherence-fast, coherence-exact
- [ ] Each subcommand wraps the matching kernel RPC / resource-tree endpoint
- [ ] CLI help + `docs/guides/` page entry
- [ ] Tests against a running daemon

## Dependencies
- T2 (`boot_ecc()` wired in) helps but isn't strictly required — RPCs already exist
- Stream 4 (weave) cross-cut

## Notes
Low effort, high operator-UX value once ECC is being exercised.

name: ws17: ECC — define new RVF segment types for ECC structures and persistence
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T7, D5)

## Problem / gap

ECC structures (CausalEdge, ImpulseRecord, CalibrationProfile, etc.) are conceptual at the RVF segment level — no formal segment-type definitions, so persistence is ad-hoc.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Define RVF segment types for the core ECC structures
- [ ] Add encode/decode round-trip tests
- [ ] Document in the RVF / chain reference docs

## Notes
Follow-up to the K3c chain RVF breakage repair. Non-blocking for 0.7.0.

name: ws17: ECC — resolve 5 pre-existing clippy warnings in agent_loop, chain, gate
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T8, D6)

## Problem / gap

5 pre-existing clippy warnings remain at:
- `agent_loop.rs:30,138,219`
- `chain.rs:1239`
- `gate.rs:427`

Not introduced by ECC, but unaddressed and surfaced during the K3c work.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 5 warnings either fixed or explicitly `#[allow(...)]` with a justification comment
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh clippy` clean

## Dependencies
- None

## Notes
P3 cleanup; group with similar lint-debt items.

name: ws17: EML — incremental component-count maintenance for O(1) coherence feature extraction
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T9, D7)
- `crates/clawft-kernel/src/eml_coherence.rs` (`from_causal_graph`)

## Problem / gap

`from_causal_graph` is currently O(n+m) because `connected_components()` is recomputed each call. For true O(1) feature extraction the component count needs incremental maintenance on edge add/remove.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add union-find or equivalent incremental connected-components structure to `CausalGraph`
- [ ] `from_causal_graph` runs in amortised O(1) for the component-count feature
- [ ] Benchmarks confirm the speed-up (`scripts/build.sh` integrate if needed)
- [ ] Existing EML coherence tests still pass

## Dependencies
- `causal.rs`

## Notes
Key for keeping the Democritus fast tick under budget at scale.

name: ws17: EML — Iteration 1 end-to-end coordinate-descent loop for Q/K/V models
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T10, D8, D10)
- `crates/eml-core/src/attention.rs`

## Problem / gap

In `ToyEmlAttention` (Iteration 0), Q/K/V models remain at default initialization — only the output projections get the EML treatment. Iteration 1 needs an end-to-end coordinate-descent loop training all five EML models jointly. This subsumes D10 (coord-descent on multiple interdependent EML models is an open problem).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Design + ADR for joint coordinate-descent across QKV+output EML models
- [ ] Implementation behind `experimental-attention` feature
- [ ] Convergence demonstrated on at least one toy task
- [ ] Numerical-stability notes captured

## Dependencies
- Existing `EmlModel` training, `ToyEmlAttention`

## Notes
Research-grade item; speculative.

name: ws17: EML — drive top 5 eml-synergy-scan rows from scan to implementation
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T11, D9)
- `.planning/development_notes/eml-synergy-scan.md`

## Problem / gap

Scan catalogued dozens of hardcoded heuristics (graphify analyze surprise scorer L209-269, cluster `MAX_COMMUNITY_FRACTION`, ForceAtlas2 6-parameter physics tuner, HNSW ef-adaptive beam, peripheral-hub detection L254-255, bridge-node `.take(3)` cut, HTML viz physics constants L246-251 + 253). Most rows are still hardcoded.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Pick top 5 by impact/feasibility and document the choice in `eml-synergy-scan.md`
- [ ] Replace each with an `EmlModel` (or principled alternative) and add tests
- [ ] Cross-stream items into Stream 12 (graphify) where applicable

## Dependencies
- `eml-core` training APIs
- Stream 12 (graphify)

## Notes
Good candidate for a focused 0.8.x mini-sprint.

name: ws17: KG — RoMem phase-rotation temporal KG on CausalGraph
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T12, D41)
- `.planning/development_notes/knowledge-graph-paper-survey-phase2.md` (RoMem, arXiv:2604.11544)

## Problem / gap

RoMem proposes phase-rotation temporal KG to replace hard age-pruning with geometric shadowing on CausalEdge. Sketched and prioritised, no code.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ADR sketching phase-rotation embedding for `CausalEdge`
- [ ] Reference implementation behind a feature flag
- [ ] Comparison vs current hard-deletion pruning on a real workload

## Dependencies
- `causal.rs`, `eml_coherence.rs`
- Cross-cuts Stream 12

## Notes
P1 in audit; cycle 0.9.x because it's research-grade and not gating any shipped feature.

name: ws17: KG — CausalRAG causal_trace() over typed edges
description_md:

name: ws17: KG — SASE clustering replacing label-propagation in cluster.rs
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T15, D40)
- `.planning/development_notes/knowledge-graph-paper-survey.md` (SASE, arXiv:2408.05765)

## Problem / gap

SASE (k-order graph convolution + Random Fourier Features) is parameter-free, linear-time, and would replace the current label-propagation in `cluster.rs`. Sketched, not implemented.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Implement SASE behind a feature flag in `cluster.rs`
- [ ] Benchmark vs label-propagation on representative graphs
- [ ] Migrate default once parity is shown
- [ ] Update Stream 12 docs

## Dependencies
- `cluster.rs`
- Stream 12

## Notes
Largely a graphify item; ws17 sponsors the research.

name: ws17: KG — LightRAG dual-level keyword retrieval
description_md:

LightRAG (Guo et al. 2410.05779) — dual-level keyword retrieval, ~610× fewer tokens than GraphRAG; would slot into `suggest_questions()`. Not in code.

name: ws17: KG — process remaining Phase 2 papers into priority list
description_md:

- `.planning/development_notes/knowledge-graph-paper-survey-phase2.md`

~5 remaining 2604.* Phase 2 papers are filed but not yet priority-tagged or fed into the implementation queue.

## Acceptance criteria

name: ws17: LeWM — codify ADR-058 decoupling-invariant runtime checks
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T18, D35, Q4)
- `.planning/symposiums/lewm-worldmodel/diagram.md`
- ADR-058

## Problem / gap

ADR-058 states the decoupling invariant — ECC remains authoritative per node, the latent world model is an additive consumer. The five formal rules are documented in prose but there are no compile-time or runtime checks that would catch a violation (e.g. world model short-circuiting a causal edge).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Enumerate the five rules as testable predicates in an ADR addendum
- [ ] Add runtime assertions / debug checks inside the world-model facade
- [ ] Add a property test that demonstrates a forbidden short-circuit is rejected
- [ ] Update `lewm-worldmodel-rs` page if user-facing

## Dependencies
- LeWM crates (if implemented). Can land as text-only ADR + check stubs first

## Notes
ADR-only is acceptable for 0.8.x; runtime checks land alongside the implementation crates in 0.9.x.

name: ws17: LeWM — create weftos-worldmodel-core crate (no_std traits)
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T19, D19)
- `.planning/lewm-worldmodel-rs-page/{DESIGN-A,DESIGN-B,PLAN}.md`
- `.planning/symposiums/lewm-worldmodel/diagram.md`
- ADR-048 → ADR-058

## Problem / gap

The LeWM diagram and ADR batch describe a `weftos-worldmodel-core` `no_std` traits crate. Crate does not exist on master at 2026-04-28.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Crate scaffolded under `crates/weftos-worldmodel-core/`
- [ ] Core traits defined: encoder, predictor, planner, lattice API surface
- [ ] `no_std` build verified
- [ ] Stub tests pass
- [ ] Workspace `Cargo.toml` updated

## Dependencies
- ADR-048 → ADR-058 finalised

## Notes
First crate of the LeWM 7-crate batch; foundation for impls + facade.

name: ws17: LeWM — create weftos-worldmodel-impls crate (candle ViT-tiny + AdaLN)
description_md:

- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T20, D20)
- `.planning/symposiums/lewm-worldmodel/diagram.md`

## Problem / gap

Candle-backed ViT-tiny encoder + AdaLN-modulated predictor implementations are described in the diagram and ADRs but have no crate on master.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Crate scaffolded under `crates/weftos-worldmodel-impls/`
- [ ] Candle-backed ViT-tiny encoder and AdaLN predictor stubs that satisfy the core traits
- [ ] End-to-end SIGReg implementation (Epps-Pulley on M random 1D projections), no EMA, no stop-gradient

- T19 (`weftos-worldmodel-core`)
- candle dep coupling decision

## Notes
Largest implementation crate; ADR-050 SIGReg manifold is the spine.

name: ws17: LeWM — create weftos-worldmodel facade crate
description_md:

Sensor pipeline + on-the-wire crates (CBOR + Ed25519, observational-only packets, ExoChain-indexed every frame) do not exist on master.

name: ws17: LeWM — implement LatticeApi (7 methods) via ServiceApi

`LatticeApi` is registered in spec only. Required methods: observe, observe_node, predict, plan, recall, subscribe_surprise, subscribe_drift.

name: ws17: LeWM — wire SIGReg sigreg_health Welford monitor + auto-rollback at 0.85/30s
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T27, D28, D29)
- ADR-050

## Problem / gap

ADR-050 mandates Welford-based `sigreg_health` measurable in production, version-tagged, ExoChain-attested, with auto-rollback when `sigreg_health < 0.85` for 30 s. None of this exists in code.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Welford running stats wired into the encoder/predictor pipeline
- [ ] `sigreg_health` exposed as a metric and logged to ExoChain
- [ ] Auto-rollback path triggered by 30s window of unhealthy readings
- [ ] Tests forcing low health and confirming rollback

## Dependencies
- T20 (`weftos-worldmodel-impls`)
- ExoChain attestation hookup (T32)

## Notes
Directly governs production safety of the world model.

name: ws17: LeWM — implement pred_φ predictor + LatentPlanner (CEM default)
description_md:

- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T28, D30, D31)

## Problem / gap

No `pred_φ` (z_t, a_t → ẑ_{t+1}) predictor. No `LatentPlanner` (CEM default / MPPI-warm / gradient shooting) running on the 10 Hz background thread.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `pred_φ` predictor implemented behind the core trait
- [ ] `LatentPlanner` with CEM default; MPPI / gradient as configurable variants
- [ ] 10 Hz background-thread integration in `clawft-worldmodel-service`
- [ ] Tests covering one toy planning task

## Dependencies
- T19, T20, T23, T26

## Notes
Core real-time loop component.

name: ws17: LeWM — implement four-condition AND rollback gate
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T29, D32)
- ADR-055

## Problem / gap

ADR-055 specifies a four-condition AND gate: cluster SIGReg health, held-out probing accuracy, VoE surprise differentiation, temporal-straightening score. None implemented.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All four metrics computed in production paths
- [ ] AND-combined gate that blocks promotion when any condition fails
- [ ] Tests covering each condition's veto independently
- [ ] Doc page entry

## Dependencies
- T20 (impls), T27 (SIGReg health)

## Notes
Pair with T30 (training surfaces).

name: ws17: LeWM — implement two training surfaces (offline edge + online streaming-merge)
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T30, D33)
- ADR-055

## Problem / gap

ADR-055 defines two surfaces: (1) offline per-sensor-class edge intelligence (RVF-delivered, hot-swappable); (2) online streaming-merge world-model training with per-class importance-weighted replay. Neither in code.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Offline edge-intelligence pipeline: RVF segment delivery + hot-swap at tick alignment
- [ ] Online streaming-merge trainer with importance-weighted replay buffer
- [ ] Hooked into the four-condition AND rollback gate
- [ ] Tests for one cycle of each surface

## Dependencies
- T29 (rollback gate)
- RVF segment infra

## Notes
P2 in audit; cycle 0.9.x. Heavy item.

name: ws17: LeWM — per-sensor-class trainable RVF-hosted small models with hot-swap
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T31, D37)
- ADR-057

## Problem / gap

ADR-057 defines three trainable RVF-hosted small models per sensor class (transmit-gate, aggregate, encode), hot-swap at tick alignment, with auto-rollback. Not implemented.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] RVF segment types for the three model variants
- [ ] Hot-swap mechanism aligned to tick boundaries
- [ ] Auto-rollback hooked into the AND gate
- [ ] Tests covering swap + rollback cycle

## Notes
Pairs with the training surfaces work.

description_md:

Marketing/explainer page is scaffolded (18 client/RSC components, motion v12, blueprint palette, prefers-reduced-motion branch) but PR posture is 'DO NOT MERGE — awaiting visual confirmation'.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Perf budget verified: ≤25 KB gz motion JS, ≤80 KB gz total route, ≤40 KB SVG, LCP < 2.0 s, CLS = 0, scroll INP < 120 ms
- [ ] Page merged to development branch and deployed
- [ ] PLAN.md acceptance checklist closed

- Designer review
- Stream docs

## Notes
Pure docs/marketing — independent of crate work.

name: ws17: docs — decide on single research → feature pipeline index vs ADR-only
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T40, Q7)

## Problem / gap

The audit found no single index of research → feature transitions. Each ADR currently serves as its own marker, which is workable but makes audit-time discovery painful.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded: single index file vs ADR-only
- [ ] If single index: scaffold and seed with the existing landed-as-feature entries (ECC, EML, Democritus, Exochain governance)
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md / handoff if owner-facing convention changes

## Dependencies
- Docs owner

## Notes
Governance-of-research item.

name: ws17: ECC — decide boot_ecc() fold-vs-fork into Kernel
description_md:
## Source - `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T41, Q1) ## Problem / gap Open architectural question: should `boot_ecc()` be folded into the standard `boot()` flow, or remain a feature-flag-gated fork? The choice affects `Kernel

` struct shape. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] Decision recorded in an ADR or kernel-architecture note - [ ] Implementation strategy for T2 aligned with the decision ## Dependencies - ECC feature-flag policy ## Notes Prerequisite to T2.

name: ws17: LeWM — decide 192-dim SIGReg latent dimensionality (ADR-050)
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (T42, Q2)
- ADR-050

## Problem / gap

The diagram fixes 192 dims for the SIGReg manifold. This binds the wire format. We need a documented decision before the wire ships, not after.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ADR-050 amended (or addended) with the rationale for 192 dims, including the encoder-model assumptions
- [ ] Wire format pinned in `weftos-sensor-pipeline-wire` once T22 lands
- [ ] Migration / version-bump policy noted

## Dependencies
- T22 (sensor-pipeline-wire)

## Notes
Must precede `mesh.sensor.v1.*` topic ship (T25).

name: ws17: governance — decide rotate-but-not-revoke policy expression for auth_service
description_md:

## Problem / gap

With auth_service gates landing (T4), we need a policy expression for cases like 'agent X may rotate but not revoke'. Today's policy DSL is unclear on this dimension.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Document the canonical policy expression
- [ ] Wire test cases into the auth_service test suite

## Dependencies

- Stream 8

Policy/governance design item.

name: ws17: EML — numerical-stability scaffolding for nested exp/ln at scale
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` (D11)

## Problem / gap

The CIFAR-10 MLP run blew up on deep EML trees. Full LLM-scale would require heroic numerical-stability engineering. Today there is no framework-level stability layer in `eml-core`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Catalogue the numerical issues (over/underflow loci) in `eml-core`
- [ ] Prototype a stability layer (log-sum-exp tricks, range clamping, AdaScale-style normalization)
- [ ] Demonstrate convergence on the prior CIFAR-10 reproduction
- [ ] Document the layer in the EML guide

## Dependencies
- `eml-core`

## Notes
Research-grade; cycle 0.9.x.

name: ws17: orphans — triage OpenFang gap targets (channel breadth, Hands, Tauri, security stack, OFP, marketplace, SDKs, TUI, migration)
description_md:
## Source
- `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/17-research-streams.md` ('Orphaned research')
- `.planning/development_notes/openfang-comparison.md`

## Problem / gap

OpenFang comparison surfaced ~10 distinct gap targets that no workstream owns: channel breadth (40 vs 13), autonomous Hands agents, Tauri 2.0 desktop, 16-layer security stack, OpenAI-compatible API, P2P OFP wire protocol, agent marketplace, JS+Python SDKs, ratatui TUI, migration tools.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] For each of the ~10 gap targets, assign a workstream owner or explicitly mark 'no-op'
- [ ] Update `openfang-comparison.md` with the triage outcome
- [ ] Cross-link any items that become real tasks into their owning streams

## Dependencies
- Workstream owners

## Notes
These are competitive-analysis items, not research. Filed under ws17 because the comparison report lives there.
